title: Eating Out Budget Check
body: You've been spending heavily on food. Every $50 week of eating out adds up to $2,600/year. Setting a $30/week dining budget saves you $1,040 — enough for your emergency fund and more.

title: Subscription Stack Check
body: Multiple streaming, app, and delivery subscriptions can hit $80–120/month without you noticing. Take 5 minutes to list yours. Cancel anything you've used less than once a week.

title: Shopping Cart Pause
body: Before you checkout, leave items in your cart for 48 hours. If you still want them, buy them. This one rule reduces impulse purchases by 30–50% for most people.

title: Social Spending Reality Check
body: FOMO spending is real. Before saying yes to an expensive plan, ask: can I suggest a cheaper alternative? 'Let's cook together instead' is always an option — and often more fun.

title: Beauty Budget Awareness
body: Beauty and self-care spending adds up fast. Not a judgment — just an awareness moment. Try setting a monthly cap ($30–50) and shopping the dupes for one item this month.

title: Transport Spending Review
body: Rideshare and transport costs often go unnoticed. Check last month's total. If it's over $80, explore: campus shuttle, bike share, or carpooling. Small swaps = big savings.
